system.summary: Shard Cards are FragPunk's signature mechanic: per-round modifier cards that change the rules. At the start of each round your team is offered 3 randomly drawn cards (from your collected deck); you spend Shard Points to activate them. Effects buff your team, nerf enemies, or alter the map/weapons.
system.points: Shard Points are earned from performance (kills, objectives) and pooled by the team. Each card shows its cost at the top. A card showing 'N' instead of a number is a VARIABLE card -- its effect scales with how many points you pour into it.
system.draft: You only see 3 cards per round, drawn from your own collection, so building a focused deck means you see your best cards more often.
strategy: Read the round: pick cards that answer what's hurting you (anti-armor vs heavy buys, mobility vs a turtling defense, healing vs poke damage).
Coordinate as a team -- Shard Points are shared, so agree on which card(s) to fund instead of splitting points across several.
Economy/value cards compound over a half; tempo cards win one specific round -- spend accordingly.
Note which cards the enemy activated (shown each round) so you can counter-pick next round.
Variable ('N') cards are flexible: dump points when you have a surplus, skip when saving for a key card.

categories.weapon_damage: Modify guns/damage: e.g. Big Heads (bigger enemy head hitboxes), Explosive Bullets, Chain Lightning, Ricochet, Vampire Bullet (lifesteal).
categories.map_environment: Transform the arena: Tall Grass (stealth/cover everywhere), Floor is Lava (ground damage), Flash Fog (low visibility, favors close range).
categories.survivability: Stay alive: extra HP/armor, healing, revive/respawn effects (e.g. Bio-Warrior -- return as a zombie).
categories.mobility_utility: Movement & tricks: speed, teleports, wall-pierce (Reaper's Scythe), vision/recon.
categories.team_comp: Composition rule changes: Clone Tech (multiple teammates pick the SAME Lancer to stack abilities).

notable.name: Big Heads
notable.effect: Enlarges enemy head hitboxes -- much easier headshots. Strong if your team hits heads.

notable.name: Vampire Bullet
notable.effect: Lifesteal on hits -- great for aggressive duelists and sustained fights.

notable.name: Explosive Bullets
notable.effect: Shots deal area-of-effect explosive damage -- punishes grouped enemies.

notable.name: Chain Lightning
notable.effect: Bullets arc lightning between nearby enemies -- value when they cluster.

notable.name: Ricochet
notable.effect: Bullets bounce off surfaces -- corner/area denial and odd angles.

notable.name: Tall Grass
notable.effect: Covers the map in vegetation -- stealth and broken sniper sightlines.

notable.name: Floor is Lava
notable.effect: Touching the ground deals damage over time -- forces movement/positioning play.

notable.name: Flash Fog
notable.effect: Cuts overall visibility -- favors close-quarters Lancers and shotguns.

notable.name: Bio-Warrior
notable.effect: On death you return as a zombie -- extra value/pressure from a trade.

notable.name: Reaper's Scythe
notable.effect: Shoot through walls and teleport -- aggressive flanks and wall-bang picks.

notable.name: Clone Tech
notable.effect: Multiple teammates can pick the same Lancer -- stack one Lancer's abilities for a devastating combo.

note: System + categories + notable cards verified from online FragPunk references (169 cards exist in-game; this is a curated subset, not the full list). Used to ground the coach's card advice -- no guessing.
source: online FragPunk shard-card guides (PlayerAuctions, 1v9, MitchCactus, GameRant) cross-referenced
